title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Four Vehicles Total
summary: After launch, Tesla added vehicles in Dallas and Houston, reaching two unsupervised vehicles per city as of April 20. Austin shows 13 unsupervised vehicles active in the last 30 days for comparison.
impact_reason: Indicates initial fleet buildup in new cities following expansion, mildly supporting scaling velocity through early operational growth in unsupervised mode.

title: Tesla Expands Unsupervised Robotaxi to Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla launched unsupervised Robotaxi service in Dallas and Houston on April 18, adding two new cities to its Texas operations alongside Austin. Initial geofences cover compact areas in each city, with video evidence showing no safety drivers.
impact_reason: Expands unsupervised operations to two additional Texas cities, tripling the state's active hubs and building momentum for broader H1 2026 rollout, though initial deployments are small.

title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

title: Tesla's Austin Fleet at 46 Active Vehicles Amid Expansion
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data logs 46 active vehicles in Austin as Tesla expands to Dallas and Houston. The service shifted to unsupervised rides in Austin in January 2026.
impact_reason: Provides current benchmark of meaningful unsupervised fleet size in Austin, offering positive context for Texas scaling as expansions leverage this base.

title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Fleet Reaches 10 Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet achieved 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, marking the highest confirmed count of driverless vehicles in the service.
impact_reason: Mildly positive incremental progress in Austin unsupervised fleet growth, but the modest absolute number (10 vehicles) remains far below stated targets and demonstrates slow scaling velocity for the flagship market.

title: Elon Musk Announces Austin Robotaxi Fleet to Double to ~60 Vehicles
summary: Elon Musk stated the Austin Robotaxi fleet, currently at 29 supervised vehicles per community tracker, will double to about 60 next month. This falls short of the Q3 2025 goal of 500 vehicles by year-end.
impact_reason: Doubling fleet size signals positive growth trajectory in Austin but remains modest and supervised, limiting unsupervised scaling velocity.
